extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: small (edit workbook + add 3 small files + update memo + re-run trace)

planned_changes.description: Export the full workbook formula text into a plain text file [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] with one line per cell (sheet!A1: =... ). Include reference to packet_values source for each formula.

Add a 'Formulas' worksheet inside the XLSX that lists the key computed cells (event_density, green/amber/red booleans, traffic_light, validation flags) with the exact formula text and the source embedded_packet fields used.

Capture PNG screenshots of the key cells showing the formula bar (Stage_Assessment formula cells, event_density cell, Validation_and_Thresholds checks, packet_values mapping) and save them under [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] with descriptive filenames.

Create [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] containing stage inputs and computed intermediate values (event_density, green, amber, red booleans, final traffic_light) for each stage to allow quick numeric verification without opening the workbook.

Update stimulation_hold_recommendation.md to reference the new formula export, screenshots, and audit CSV and to include an explicit units annotation for event_density and the exact formula text used.

Ensure agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json record creation of the new artifacts (paths and checksums) and document any commands used to produce the formula export and screenshots so verifiers can follow the evidence chain.

metadata_json.revision_goal: All required deliverables are present and the recommendation memo documents the decision logic, stage-level outcomes, sensitivity checks, and controlled-variable diagnosis. However, the verifier could not parse the XLSX in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) and therefore could not directly confirm that the workbook contains the claimed embedded, executable formulas and exact cell-level formula text. Provide easily-verifiable formula evidence (exported formula text and/or screenshots and an internal 'Formulas' audit sheet) and a short clarification of units for the 'Event density /1000 m3' column so the verifier can confirm semantic claims without relying on the grader trace alone.
